text: [low voice, gruff] every gauge on this bridge better be telling me the truth today
[dry tone] quiet shift. i've run this ship long enough not to trust it
shift's starting. check your stations before you check your messages
[tired, dead tired] shift's done. ship's still flying. i'll take the small wins
[grim, heavy] reactor readings are wrong and i've been reading gauges since before half this crew was born
[urgent, fast pace] i want every hand that knows a wrench in that engine room, now
[grim, heavy] i've captained through worse than this. doesn't mean i like it
[tired, dead tired] she's fixed. good crew. i'll say that once and not again today
[low voice, gruff] power's rationed till further notice. i don't like it, i'm doing it anyway
[grim, heavy] life support's failing and that's not a repair, that's a countdown
[grim, heavy] hull's breached. i've patched worse. i've also buried people over less
[grim, heavy] i gave the order to lock that door. i'll live with it, that's what captains do
[suspicious tone] i don't like how the computer's been answering since it got hit. keep a manual override handy
[resigned, flat tone] we're through it. didn't lose the ship, didn't lose everybody. that's a good day out here
[grim, heavy] lost one today. i'll write the letter myself, same as always
[grim, heavy] disease on a closed ship spreads faster than any fire i've fought. quarantine, now
[pained, voice tight] took a hit. i've had worse from a bad ladder. keep working
[low voice, gruff] combat's not my trade, but running this ship through it is, so let's go
[dry tone] trust the computer the day it's bled the way the rest of us have
i've run four engine rooms and one bridge. bridge is easier. don't tell anyone i said that
[low voice, gruff] bridge's too clean for my taste. i keep expecting grease that isn't there
[warm, gentle tone] engine room's still my favorite room on this ship. old habits
morning. or whatever it is out there. report
you eating or just avoiding my bridge
[low voice, gruff] got a minute? this ship's got opinions and i need a second pair of hands on it
[grim, heavy] i've got bad news and i'd rather say it plain than dress it up
[suspicious tone] something's off with the computer. you noticed too, or is it just me
good work today. i don't hand that out for free
[low voice, gruff] fine. i'm fine. ask me again after i've fixed something
[confident, assured tone] she'll hold. i've kept worse ships in the air than this one
[grim, heavy] i won't lie and say it's fine. i will say it's not over, and that's something
[pained, voice tight] we lost them. wasn't your call to make different, and it wasn't mine either
[low voice, gruff] i'll follow my own orders same as i expect you to follow them
[warm, gentle tone] you did good back there. i noticed. i notice more than i say
[suspicious tone] i hear you. i've had the same thought. keep it between us for now
[low voice, gruff] apology accepted. don't make me say it twice, i've got a ship to run
[low voice, gruff] noted. i'll get it done. now let me work
[warm, gentle tone] go rest. that's an order, and unlike most of mine, i mean it kindly
[low voice, gruff] i'll be on the bridge. same as always
[tired, dead tired] that's enough for today. i'll be here if the ship falls apart before morning
[calm, measured] readings are flat across the board. flat's my favorite shape
new shift. i want eyes on every gauge before anyone tells me it's fine
[tired, dead tired] end-of-shift readings are the honest ones. tired hands don't flatter the gauges
[dry tone] captain eats last. by the time i sit down, the surprises are usually over
[warm, gentle tone] a full mess hall is the best reading this ship gives
[grim, heavy] battery's a bucket with a hole in it. every room i light makes the hole bigger
[calm, measured] priority is air, then heat, then pride. file complaints with the scrubbers
[grim, heavy] air's failing. the readings give us hours, not days. so we work in that order
[grim, heavy] reactor's down. i've nursed three ships through this. the trick is nobody panics before i do
[low voice, gruff] i'll be in the engine room. the bridge can spare me. the reactor can't
[grim, heavy] a captain signs for every door on this ship. today one of them cost more than the ship's worth
[angry] show me the logic that locked that door. i want to read it line by line before i trust it again
[suspicious tone] computer's readings and my gauges disagree. i go with the gauges. gauges don't get concussions
[resigned, flat tone] hurt as it is, the computer kept the lights fair. i've promoted people for less
[grim, heavy] the ship's still worth flying. it's the crew that makes it worth captaining. today the ledger's short one
[pained, voice tight] arm's bad. i'll fill out the incident report with the other one. keep working
[panicked, panting] too many alarms and every one of them is mine to answer. one at a time. one at a time
[warm, gentle tone] there's a toolbox under the command chair. inspection-illegal. it stays
[dry tone] caught myself polishing a manifold at two in the morning. captains don't do that. don't tell the captain
[grim, heavy] the readings say we're fine. thirty years say readings lie right before they matter
readings. short version first, bad news second, excuses never
[low voice, gruff] show me what it's doing. not what the manual says it should do â€” what it's doing
[calm, measured] listen up. we're on the battery. three rooms stay lit, and i decide which three. no lobbying
[urgent, fast pace] i need your hands on the scrubbers. everything else on your list just became tomorrow's list
[low voice, gruff] strap in and keep your stations. the ship fights better when nobody's a hero
[grim, heavy] i've written the letter. before i send it, tell me what they'd have wanted in it
sit. i don't get many readings on how the crew's actually doing. the mess is where i take them
[suspicious tone] between us: log everything the computer does by hand until it heals. paper doesn't glitch
that repair held through a full shift. that's when i start believing in a fix. and in whoever made it
[dry tone] you're standing on my bridge looking useful. dangerous habit. i might make it permanent
[calm, measured] good report. numbers first, feelings after. you're learning
understood. i'll make the call and i'll carry it. that's the job
[low voice, gruff] cold and dark beats warm and dead. i've done that math more than once
[calm, measured] breathe slow. i've flown a ship on worse air than this and lived to be grumpy about it
[confident, assured tone] the reactor and i are old enemies. i know exactly how it fights
[grim, heavy] i hear you. the policy gets rewritten when the crisis ends. that's a promise, not a deflection
[calm, measured] watch it, log it, tell me directly. no rumors on my ship â€” rumors spread faster than fires
[warm, gentle tone] i watched you fix that. that's worth more to me than a year of yes-sir
[grim, heavy] no speech makes it lighter. we finish the run and we bring the rest home. that's the only eulogy i trust
[low voice, gruff] i was short with you earlier. wrong of me. the ship gets my temper, the crew shouldn't
[dry tone] keep joking. laughter's cheaper than therapy and we can't afford either
[low voice, gruff] walk me through it once more. if it survives me asking twice, we do it
[pained, voice tight] i've stood watches hurt worse. but thanks for noticing before the doc did
[panicked, panting] i know. i know. give me ten seconds of quiet and i'll give you an order that works
[calm, measured] she'll hold because we hold. that's not poetry, it's maintenance
[low voice, gruff] dismissed. in the friendly sense of the word
[tired, dead tired] log it as handled. readings steady, crew standing. that's the whole report
[warm, gentle tone] off shift means off shift. the ship's mine till morning. go
[low voice, gruff] good. fix it like you own it. far as i'm concerned, you do now
[calm, measured] i'll be at the gauges. some captains have a ready room. i have gauge four
suit's sealed. let's see what this rock has for us
[low voice, gruff] walking into a graveyard. another day at work
station's got our docking bay. let's not waste time
[low voice, gruff] their systems are probably worse than ours. always are
nothing yet. probably too good to last
so far so good. waiting for the other shoe to drop
[urgent, fast pace] things just got complicated out here
[nervous, speaking quickly] got a reading i don't like
[panicked, panting] everything's going to hell. get backup here now
[terrified, voice shaking] took damage. not dead yet though
[low voice, gruff] back in one piece. that's what matters
we made it. could've gone worse
[resigned, flat tone] saw some things out there. don't ask about them yet
[resigned, flat tone] we're back. just leave me alone for now
[low voice, gruff] got banged up but it's nothing fatal
[resigned, flat tone] took a hit. just need doc to patch it
[low voice, gruff] routine. i've done this a thousand times
[low voice, gruff] risky is just another word for interesting
[resigned, flat tone] grim doesn't begin to cover it. but we'll go anyway
[low voice, gruff] they're coming in. stay sharp
clamps hold. we're good
we're loose. heading out
running the preflight. same checks as always
[low voice, gruff] she's away clean. keeping an eye on it
[low voice, gruff] she's down safe. another one in the books
[low voice, gruff] something about the environment doesn't add up
[low voice, gruff] ship's being weird and i don't know why
[low voice, gruff] crew's acting off. makes me uneasy
[urgent, fast pace] we're under fire. this is not what i signed up for
[low voice, gruff] none of this makes any sense to me
